creator: pfg@apache.org
text:
Hi again;

Your changes are great. I wanted to keep the .m4 change but on second 
thoughts it is better to drop the auto* dependencies as you suggest.


I found a building issue but it may be pilot error:

On FreeBSD-9.2-stable:

make CC=clang CXX=clang++

...
      [exec] /bin/sh ./libtool --tag=CC --mode=link clang  -O2 -pipe 
-march=athlon64-sse3 -fno-strict-aliasing -DOS_FREEBSD -DDSO_DLFCN 
-D_THREAD_SAFE -pthread -DCPU=\"amd64\" -m64 
-I/usr/local/openjdk6/include -I/usr/local/openjdk6/include/freebsd 
-Wall -Wstrict-prototypes  -pthread  -m64 
-L/usr/local/openjdk6/jre/lib/amd64/server  -ljvm -shared -Wl,-x -o 
libhdfs.la -rpath 
/usr/ports/devel/hadoop/work/hadoop-1.2.1/build/c++/FreeBSD-amd64-64/lib 
hdfs.lo hdfsJniHelper.lo
      [exec] /usr/bin/ld: .libs/hdfs.o: relocation R_X86_64_32 against 
`a local symbol' can not be used when making a shared object; recompile 
with -fPIC
      [exec] .libs/hdfs.o: could not read symbols: Bad value
      [exec] clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use 
-v to see invocation)
      [exec] gmake: *** [libhdfs.la] Errore 1
      [exec] clang -shared  .libs/hdfs.o .libs/hdfsJniHelper.o 
-L/usr/local/openjdk6/jre/lib/amd64/server -ljvm -march=athlon64-sse3 
-pthread -m64 -pthread -m64 -Wl,-x -Wl,-soname -Wl,libhdfs.so.0 -o 
.libs/libhdfs.so.0

BUILD FAILED
/usr/ports/devel/hadoop/work/hadoop-1.2.1/build.xml:2209: exec returned: 2

Total time: 1 minute 16 seconds
*** [do-build] Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/devel/hadoop.
*** [build] Error code 1
____


Some of my libraries were built before an ld change  which looks related 
so this may be a local issue. This does compile fine with the default 
gcc so I don't think it's a showstopper for the update.

Removing the first suspicious patch you mentioned and modifying the
second patch I got to the attached diff.

Thanks for the huge help!

Pedro.
